name: Restore Grouped Rows From 2 Back To 4 On The Compact Two-Stage PTX Ring

summary.name: Restore Grouped Rows From 2 Back To 4 On The Compact Two-Stage PTX Ring
summary.hypothesis: The grouped_rows=2 retest kept the recovered register budget but clearly reintroduced the long_scoreboard problem on the compact two-stage surface. Restoring grouped_rows to 4 should recover the better row-order reuse pattern from the earlier PTX anchor without reopening the losing round-6 split logic.
summary.expected_bottleneck: Long-scoreboard pressure from the grouped_rows=2 launch order is now the most specific local bottleneck to remove.
summary.risk: Low. This is a single-parameter launch-order restore on the currently healthy compact two-stage PTX surface.
ranking_notes: This is the highest-confidence next move because it directly targets the one metric that snapped back hardest on the compact two-stage surface: long_scoreboard.
It preserves the recovered 202-register budget and 22,016 B shared-memory footprint instead of reopening broader pipeline surgery.
If it still cannot beat the current 25.46 ms accepted base, the compact PTX family is likely plateaued and the next branch should be either barrier trim or 256x128.
summary.stop_condition: Stop if restoring grouped_rows to 4 requires any change beyond the hot-band launch-order constant and its immediate grouping math.
expected_local_changes: Leave the compact two-stage PTX ring unchanged.
Re-measure whether the long_scoreboard rebound disappears without giving back the recovered register budget.

notes: Build passed. Node A must measure this implementation next.
